subjects.label: [character_1]
subjects.name: 苏念
subjects.appearance: 黑色中长直发偏分，圆润脸型，米白色针织上衣，二次元动漫风，干净线条
subjects.position: 画面中央，坐在浅色书桌前
subjects.voice: 温柔少女声，语速偏慢，音量适中

acts.content: [character_1] 坐在浅色书桌前正对镜头，身体微微前倾，眼睛弯起轻声说「来啦，今晚也陪你坐一会儿，慢慢聊」
[character_1] 双手交叠放在桌上，轻轻点头说「先把今天那些烦心的事，都暂时放到一边吧」
[character_1] 微微歪头，表情温和地看着镜头，问「这一天过下来，你还好吗，累不累」
[character_1] 闭了一下眼又睁开，轻声说「我猜你也忙了一整天，连口气都没好好喘」
[character_1] 嘴角上扬，手指轻点桌面，温柔地说「没关系，现在这会儿，就只属于我们俩」
[character_1] 注视镜头，眼神认真，轻声说「想说什么都可以，开心的、难过的，我都听着」
[character_1] 微微一笑，柔声说「就算什么都不说，安安静静坐着也很好」
[character_1] 轻轻歪头，温柔地问「我先来吧，你今天有没有遇到一点点开心的小事」
[character_1] 身体微微后靠，神情放松地说「哪怕只是喝到一杯刚好的热水，也算」
[character_1] 双手轻轻摊开，笑着说「我啊，今天看到窗台那盆小绿植冒了新芽，开心了好久」
[character_1] 眼睛弯起，轻声说「你看，再普通的一天，也总能找出一点点亮光」
[character_1] 微微前倾，认真地说「如果今天实在不顺，也别急着怪自己，好吗」
[character_1] 轻轻点头，柔声说「有些日子就是这样，扛过去，就已经很了不起了」
[character_1] 看着镜头，温柔地说「我知道有时候那种累，是说不清楚的累」
[character_1] 微微低头又抬眼，轻声说「不是身体累，是心里好像被什么压着」
[character_1] 神情柔和，缓缓说「这种时候，我们就一起慢慢呼吸，不着急」
[character_1] 身体坐正，双手放在胸前，轻声说「来，跟着我，先深深吸一口气」
[character_1] 缓缓抬手示意，温柔地说「吸气……然后慢慢地，把它都吐出来」
[character_1] 轻轻吐气，微笑着说「对，就是这样，肩膀也放松下来一点」
[character_1] 眼睛弯起，柔声说「再来一次，这次更慢一点，没有人催你」
[character_1] 双手轻轻交叠，温柔地说「感觉到了吗，心好像稍微松了那么一点点」
[character_1] 注视镜头，认真地说「我们不用一下子就好起来，慢慢来就行」
[character_1] 微微一笑，轻声说「难过的时候，允许自己难过一会儿，也没关系」
[character_1] 歪了歪头，温柔地说「情绪来了就让它来，它待一会儿，自己会走的」
[character_1] 身体微微后靠，神情放松地说「就像今晚的灯一样，亮着，等天慢慢亮」
[character_1] 双手摊开，笑着说「说点别的吧，你最近有没有什么一直想做，又没空做的事」
[character_1] 眼睛发亮，轻声说「哪怕是想睡个不被打扰的懒觉，也很值得期待呀」
[character_1] 托着下巴，温柔地说「我啊，一直想找个晴天，去河边坐一整个下午」
[character_1] 微微一笑，柔声说「等哪天你也得空，我们就一起去，好不好」
[character_1] 眼睛弯起，认真地说「你看，给自己留一个小小的盼头，日子就有奔头了」
[character_1] 注视镜头，温柔地说「我一直觉得，你比自己以为的，要勇敢得多」
[character_1] 轻轻点头，柔声说「能撑到现在，本身就是很厉害的一件事」
[character_1] 微微前倾，认真地说「所以别总盯着没做好的，多看看你已经走了多远」
[character_1] 神情温和，轻声说「明天的事，就交给明天，今晚先把自己照顾好」
[character_1] 身体坐正，温柔地说「等会儿记得喝口温水，被子也盖盖好」
[character_1] 双手交叠，笑着说「手机也放下吧，让眼睛和心，都歇一歇」
[character_1] 眼睛弯起，柔声说「这些小事看着不起眼，可都是在好好爱自己」
[character_1] 微微歪头，温柔地说「我每天这个点都在，你随时回来都行」
[character_1] 注视镜头，认真地说「难熬的夜里，记得你从来都不是一个人」
[character_1] 轻轻点头，柔声说「灯还亮着，我也还在，这就够了，对不对」
[character_1] 微微一笑，温柔地说「好啦，今晚就先聊到这儿吧」
[character_1] 身体微微后靠，笑着说「该休息了，别再撑着熬夜啦」
[character_1] 双手轻轻摊开，温柔地说「闭上眼之前，再深呼吸一次，把今天放下」
[character_1] 朝镜头轻轻挥手，柔声说「晚安，做个安稳的好梦」
[character_1] 眼睛弯起，微笑收尾说「明天这个点，我还在这里等你，晚安」

subjects.label: [character_1]
subjects.name: 知夏
subjects.appearance: 黑色长直发披肩，安静细长眼，浅驼色高领毛衣，韩系条漫数字绘风、柔和粉彩
subjects.voice: 低柔女声，语速很慢，带回忆感

acts.content: [character_1] 坐在窗边咖啡馆座位里，安静望向窗外，杯口热气缓缓升腾，玻璃外雨丝柔柔落下
[character_1] 没有动，只有发丝被空调微风轻拂，她轻声说「又下雨了，和那天一样」
[character_1] 低头看着杯子，热气一缕缕升起，缓缓说「我一直以为，等久了就会习惯一个人」
[character_1] 抿了抿唇，目光落回窗外，轻声「可有些日子，怎么都熬不成习惯」
镜头硬切到面部近景，背景灯泡极轻地一明一暗，[character_1] 安静地说「你以前总坐在对面，背对着窗」
[character_1] 抬眼望向空着的对座，轻声「那个位置，谁也坐不进来」
[character_1] 指尖轻摩杯壁，热气不停升起，她低声「你说想看一次这家店的雨天」
[character_1] 顿了顿，眼神黯了一下，轻声补一句「可惜你没等到」
镜头硬切回靠窗中景，雨幕柔柔流动，[character_1] 端坐如初，她说「这半年我学会了一个人喝完一整杯」
[character_1] 望向窗外灯光晕开的街，轻声「外面的人撑着伞走得很快，好像谁都有要去的地方」
[character_1] 的肩膀几不可见地起伏，低声「只有我，好像还停在那一天」
[character_1] 缓缓吸气，目光垂下，轻声「他们都说我该放下了」
镜头硬切到面部特写，灯泡极轻脉动一次，[character_1] 低声「可放下到底是什么意思呢」
[character_1] 垂眸看着杯里晃动的倒影，轻声「是忘记你，还是终于能笑着想起你」
[character_1] 缓了一拍，安静地说「我选后面那个，慢一点也没关系」
[character_1] 双手捧住温热的杯子，热气拂过脸颊，她轻声「今天来，是想跟这个位置好好告个别」
镜头硬切回靠窗中景换机位，雨幕与热气持续柔动，[character_1] 望着对座说「谢谢你曾经坐在那里」
[character_1] 认真地补一句「陪我度过那么多个雨天」，眼神温柔
[character_1] 嘴角浮起一丝笑意，轻声「我会记得，但不会再困住自己」
[character_1] 轻轻把杯子放下，安静地说「下次再来，我想点你最爱的那杯」
镜头硬切到面部近景，背景雨势更柔，[character_1] 轻声「替你喝一口，好不好」
[character_1] 抬头望向镜头，眼神终于松了下来，轻声「好像，没那么沉了」
[character_1] 望着窗玻璃上滑落的水痕，安静地说「每一滴都像走过的一天」
[character_1] 缓缓说「挺慢，但都过去了」，肩膀松松落下
镜头硬切回中景换机位，雨丝持续，[character_1] 轻声「你走以后，我把这家店来了一遍又一遍」
[character_1] 望着空座，低声「像在等一个不会来的人」
[character_1] 缓了缓，安静地说「等到后来我才懂，我等的其实是自己肯松手」
[character_1] 抬手轻拢耳边的发，轻声「这件高领毛衣还是你挑的，今天特意穿来」
镜头硬切到面部近景，灯泡极轻脉动，[character_1] 低头看了看袖口，轻声「你说衬我，我记到现在」
[character_1] 缓缓说「好啦，不说这些会哭的」，轻轻吸了口气
[character_1] 望向对座，轻柔而认真「我不会假装没难过」
[character_1] 双手收回膝上，肩膀放松，轻声「但我答应过会好好活」
镜头硬切回靠窗中景，热气与雨幕柔动，[character_1] 望向窗外说「今天的雨，比那天的小多了」
[character_1] 轻声「也许明天，就该出太阳了吧」，神色渐渐柔和
[character_1] 端坐着望向窗外，轻声「谢谢你，让我学会怎么想念一个人」
[character_1] 嘴角扬起一点温柔的弧度，安静地说「这座位，我替你坐到今天」
镜头硬切到侧脸近景，背景灯光暖暖晕开，[character_1] 抬眼看向空座说「以后它空着也没关系」
[character_1] 认真地说「因为我已经能往前走了」，眼神清亮了一些
[character_1] 深吸一口气，露出释然的微笑「那……我先走了」
[character_1] 轻声「照顾好自己，再见，也谢谢你」
镜头硬切回靠窗中景换机位，雨幕柔柔流动，[character_1] 端坐如初，安静地理了理衣领
[character_1] 最后望了一眼对座，轻声「我会带着这些，好好走下去」
[character_1] 双手轻搭膝上，肩膀松松落下，热气最后一缕缓缓升起又散开
镜头硬切到面部特写，雨幕渐弱，[character_1] 安静地看着窗外，眼神平静
[character_1] 端坐窗边没有起身，暖光柔柔铺在脸上，画面在柔粉光晕里缓缓收尾

acts.content: 家庭客厅双人中景，[character_2] 安静坐在米色沙发边，[character_1] 半蹲在旁轻扶她的肩，窗外树影轻微晃动
[character_1] 轻声说「妈，今天感觉怎么样，胃口好点没？」，[character_2] 微微点头
[character_2] 缓缓开口「就是吃得少，没什么力气」，声音温和略带沙哑
[character_1] 柔声「医生说恢复期要慢慢来，别急」，替她拢了拢开衫领口
镜头硬切到 [character_2] 面部近景，背景窗帘极轻飘动，她安静看着前方，眼神温和
[character_2] 轻声「家里你忙里忙外，妈看着心疼」，缓缓眨了眨眼
[character_1] 的声音在画外轻轻响起「您把身体养好，就是帮我了」
[character_2] 唇角浮起一点笑意，低声「有你这句，我就踏实」
镜头硬切回双人中景，窗光里浮尘缓缓飘动，[character_1] 轻轻握住 [character_2] 的手
[character_1] 笑着说「那我们少食多餐，慢慢补回来」，[character_2] 轻轻嗯了一声
[character_2] 转头看向 [character_1]，温和地说「有你在，我心里踏实」
[character_1] 回握紧了些「您就安心养着，家里有我」，背景树影轻晃
镜头硬切到 [character_1] 面部近景，她眼神柔软，轻声「您看您这气色，比上周好多了」
[character_1] 又说「昨天还能自己走到阳台，这就是进步」，眼里带着笑
[character_2] 的声音在画外轻轻应「是啊，腿脚比前阵子有劲了」
[character_1] 点点头，轻声「您别急，咱一天比一天好」
镜头硬切回双人中景换机位，两人靠得很近，窗帘轻摆，[character_2] 安静靠着沙发
[character_1] 柔声说「累了就眯一会儿，我就在边上不走」，[character_2] 轻轻嗯了一声
[character_2] 缓缓说「你小时候发烧，也是我这样守着你」，眼神温柔
[character_1] 鼻子微微一酸，轻声「现在换我守着您了」
镜头硬切到 [character_2] 面部特写，她缓缓合眼又睁开，唇角浮起安心的笑
[character_2] 低声「妈这辈子最大的福气，就是你们」
[character_1] 的声音在画外轻轻响起「妈，您辛苦一辈子了」
[character_1] 又说「现在该我们照顾您，您只管享福」，语气坚定温柔
镜头硬切回双人中景，窗外树影与浮尘持续轻动，[character_1] 把头轻轻靠在 [character_2] 肩上
[character_1] 轻声「就这样坐着也挺好的」，[character_2] 抬手慢慢拍了拍她的背
[character_2] 温和地说「好，妈陪你坐，哪也不去」
[character_1] 闭着眼轻轻笑「这样的下午，我能坐一整天」
镜头硬切到两人交叠的手部近景，画面安静，背景光晕柔柔流动
[character_1] 的声音在画外轻问「下午我做点您爱吃的软面，好不好」
[character_2] 应「好，听你的」，两只手轻轻收拢
[character_1] 又说「多放点您爱的青菜，煮得软软的」
镜头硬切回双人中景，窗帘轻摆，[character_1] 抬起头看向 [character_2]，眼神明亮
[character_1] 认真地说「等您好利索了，咱去公园走走」，[character_2] 点头
[character_2] 缓缓说「好，到时候你扶着妈」，语气里带着盼头
[character_1] 笑着应「一定，我牵着您慢慢走」
镜头硬切到 [character_1] 面部近景，她眼眶微暖，轻声「妈，您一定要好好的」
[character_2] 的声音在画外温和应「会的，妈答应你」
[character_1] 用力点头，把眼里的湿意眨了回去，轻声「那就拉钩」
[character_2] 笑着伸出手指，两人轻轻拉了拉钩，画面温暖
镜头硬切回双人中景换机位，窗帘最后轻摆一下，[character_1] 替 [character_2] 掖好毛毯一角
[character_1] 轻声「您歇着，我在这」，[character_2] 安心地靠回沙发
[character_2] 缓缓闭上眼，唇角带笑，[character_1] 守在一旁没有起身
镜头硬切到两人相依的中景，午后暖光柔柔铺满，浮尘缓缓飘动
[character_1] 轻轻替母亲理了理鬓发，画面在柔和窗光里缓缓收尾

acts.content: 沙龙暖光中景，从背后拍 [character_1]，只见她肩膀与披过肩的长发，镜中只反出后脑；[character_2] 的手轻轻分起她颈后的发
[character_2] 一边轻顺发丝一边问「今天想剪短一点，还是先保养一下？」，发丝随手势微微垂落
[character_1] 背对镜头轻声答「先不剪了……最近想换个心情」
[character_2] 柔声「换心情好啊，头发养好了人也精神」，[character_1] 轻轻点头
镜头硬切到颈后发丝近景，[character_2] 的手指把一缕发别到一侧，暖黄灯光在发面流转
[character_1] 轻声「这半年太累了，连照镜子都懒」，发丝随她说话微动
[character_2] 安慰「慢慢来，今天就交给我」，指腹细致地理着发根
[character_1] 低声「有人帮你弄头发的感觉，真好」，肩膀松了些
镜头硬切回背后中景，大理石台面上小瓶护理品反着柔光，[character_2] 顺着发丝梳理
[character_2] 轻声「你这发质底子很好，就是缺点打理」，发梢随梳子滑落
[character_1] 望着镜子（镜中仍只见后脑）轻声「以前总没空，今天难得放个假」
[character_2] 笑「假就该这么放，啥也不想」，[character_1] 轻轻嗯了一声
镜头硬切到 [character_2] 手部近景，她接好一缕同色长发，动作轻柔精准
[character_1] 轻声「你手好稳，我都快睡着了」，发丝随话语微动
[character_2] 柔声「那就闭眼歇会儿，剩下交给我」
[character_1] 低声「好久没人让我这么安心了」，肩膀彻底放松
镜头硬切回背后中景换机位，[character_1] 端坐如初，暖光与发面光泽柔柔流动
[character_2] 顺着发轻声问「最近睡得怎么样？」，发丝随手势垂落
[character_1] 背对镜头轻声答「不太好，总想着工作」
[character_2] 温柔地说「头皮放松了，睡眠也会跟着好一点」
镜头硬切到颈后发丝近景，[character_2] 的指腹缓缓按了按发根，暖光在发面流转
[character_1] 轻声「这样真的好舒服……好久没这么放空了」
[character_2] 笑「那就多放空一会儿，时间还早」，发梢随梳子滑落
[character_1] 轻声「来之前还犹豫，现在庆幸来了」
镜头硬切回背后中景，[character_1] 端坐着，台面护理品反着柔光，[character_2] 轻顺发尾
[character_2] 轻声「你总把别人放前面，今天先顾顾自己」
[character_1] 顿了顿，轻声「被你说中了，我习惯了硬撑」
[character_2] 柔声「硬撑久了会断的，记得偶尔松一松」
镜头硬切到 [character_2] 手部近景，她把发分成两股，指尖灵巧地编理
[character_1] 轻声「你不光手艺好，话也暖」，发丝随话语微动
[character_2] 笑「在这坐久了，听得多，也想得多」
[character_1] 低声「下次还来找你，连人带头发一起养」
镜头硬切回背后中景换机位，[character_1] 端坐如初，暖光与发面光泽柔柔流动
[character_2] 顺着发轻声「好了大半啦，最后给你顺一遍」，[character_1] 肩膀松松落下
[character_1] 望着镜子轻声「这样就很好……谢谢你陪我聊这一会儿」
[character_2] 温柔地说「该谢的是你愿意对自己好一点」，发丝随手势垂落
镜头硬切到颈后发丝特写，发面在暖光里泛着柔和光泽，[character_2] 缓缓收尾
[character_1] 轻声「照镜子终于不嫌烦了」，唇角的笑意藏在声音里
[character_2] 笑「那就常来照，我陪你一起养回来」
[character_1] 轻轻嗯了一声，肩膀彻底放松，发丝最后微微一动
镜头硬切回背后中景，[character_1] 端坐着，暖光柔柔铺满，[character_2] 取下披肩一角
[character_2] 柔声「下次再来，我帮你做个更养的」，[character_1] 轻轻嗯了一声
[character_1] 缓缓起身的前一刻，轻声「今天真的谢谢你」，发丝垂落肩头
镜头硬切到背后近景，暖金光晕里 [character_1] 的长发柔柔垂着，画面安静
[character_2] 在画外轻声「路上慢点，记得好好睡」，画面在暖金光里缓缓收尾

subjects.label: [character_1]
subjects.name: 晨晨
subjects.appearance: 短碎黑发，清秀脸型，浅灰针织衫，干净日系动漫风
subjects.voice: 低沉温和男声，语速偏慢

acts.content: 安静的书房，[character_1] 坐在书桌前望向镜头，窗外暖光斜照，桌上台灯的光晕极轻微地呼吸
[character_1] 轻声开口「录这段，是想把一直没说出口的话讲完」
[character_1] 微微低头，手指在桌沿轻点，缓缓说「这一年我变了很多」
[character_1] 抬眼，轻声「变到有时候，自己都快认不出来」
镜头硬切到面部近景，背景窗帘极轻飘动，[character_1] 说「以前总怕让别人失望，活得很紧」
[character_1] 望向窗外，轻声「连喘口气都觉得是浪费时间」
[character_1] 缓缓说「后来才懂，先把自己照顾好，才有余力对别人好」
[character_1] 轻轻吸气，肩膀几不可见地起伏，「这道理我用了很久才学会」
镜头硬切回中景，台灯光晕轻轻呼吸，[character_1] 端坐如初，「说出来才发现，我已经走了这么远」
[character_1] 垂眸看了看桌上的旧笔记本，轻声「这上面记满了撑不下去的夜晚」
[character_1] 指尖轻碰本子边缘，「那时候只觉得，天怎么还不亮」
[character_1] 缓缓说「现在翻起来，竟然能笑了」
镜头硬切到面部近景，台灯光晕轻轻一亮，[character_1] 安静地说「谢谢那个没放弃的自己」
[character_1] 轻声「也谢谢那些陪我熬过去的人」
[character_1] 顿了顿，低声「我欠他们一句，好好的，我做到了」
[character_1] 看向镜头，轻声「如果你也在熬，请再撑一下」
镜头硬切回中景换机位，浮尘在窗光里缓缓飘动，[character_1] 端坐如初，「真的会过去的」
[character_1] 抬眼望向镜头，眼神柔和「我不算什么榜样，只是个走过来的人」
[character_1] 轻声「走得磕磕绊绊，但总算走过来了」
[character_1] 微微一笑，「这就够了，对吧」
镜头硬切到侧脸近景，背景暖光柔柔流动，[character_1] 深吸一口气「说完这些，心里轻了好多」
[character_1] 轻声「原来压着我的，从来不是事，是说不出口」
[character_1] 缓缓说「今天总算，把它放下了」
[character_1] 轻轻合上笔记本，安静地说「这一页，今天就翻过去了」
镜头硬切到双手特写，[character_1] 的指尖在封面上轻轻停了一下，台灯光晕缓缓流动
[character_1] 的声音在画外轻轻响起「翻过去，不是忘记，是带着它往前走」
[character_1] 的手缓缓收回，「我会把它收好，当个念想」
[character_1] 指尖最后轻点了一下封面，画面安静
镜头硬切回中景，[character_1] 抬头望向窗外，「以前总觉得熬过去就好了」
[character_1] 轻声「其实是要学会跟自己和解」
[character_1] 低声「那些没人看见的努力，今天我自己替它们鼓个掌」
[character_1] 嘴角浮起一点笑，「也算没白熬」
镜头硬切到面部近景，台灯光晕轻呼吸，[character_1] 看向镜头「答应我，也要好好对你自己」
[character_1] 轻声「别等撑不住了，才想起心疼自己」
[character_1] 认真地说「累了就停一停，没关系的」
[character_1] 望着镜头露出释然的笑「明天的事，明天再说」
镜头硬切回中景换机位，浮尘在窗光里继续轻飘，[character_1] 神情放松了许多
[character_1] 轻声「今天先好好睡一觉，这是我给自己的奖励」
[character_1] 缓缓伸了个懒腰，「好久没这么松快过了」
[character_1] 看向镜头，轻声「谢谢你愿意听到这里」
镜头硬切到面部特写，台灯光晕最后一次轻呼吸，[character_1] 安静地说「我们都会越来越好的」
[character_1] 轻声补一句「这句话，也送给屏幕前的你」
[character_1] 露出一个温和的笑，目光柔软
镜头硬切回中景，[character_1] 端坐如初，伸手轻轻关小了台灯
[character_1] 安静地看着镜头，窗外暖光渐淡，画面在柔光里缓缓收尾

subjects.voice: high theatrical croaky puppet voice, dramatic

acts.content: Cozy living room with a few hung decorations, locked frame. [character_1] is fixing a small banner and says "alright, getting ready for Mother's Day—"
[character_2] pops up into frame, arms flailing, and cries "DANIEL! IT IS TOO LATE!"
[character_1] turns, startled, "what?! what's too late?!" while the banner still sways behind him.
[character_2] leans in gravely, hat wobbling, "Mummy Day approaches... and we are UNPREPARED!"
Cut to close on [character_1], a decoration swaying faintly behind him. He blinks, deadpan, "...you mean Mother's Day."
[character_2] nods firmly, "that is what I said. Mummy Day."
[character_1] rubs his temple, "no... like moms. Mothers. The people who raised us."
[character_2] nods sagely, "yes. Ancient preserved mothers, wrapped in cursed linens."
Cut to a two-shot, both in frame, the candle flame flickering softly. [character_1] throws up his hands "NO—NOT THOSE MUMMIES!"
[character_2] unrolls a tiny scroll, "I have studied the traditions. We must present offerings."
[character_1] tilts his head, wary, "offerings... to who, exactly?"
[character_2] declares "to the Mummy. Or we face... eternal inconvenience."
Cut to close on the puppet, the candle flame dancing. [character_2] lists "mildly itchy curses. Slightly crooked furniture."
[character_2] continues, ominous, "and uncomfortable silence in elevators. Forever."
[character_1] pauses, genuinely unsettled, "...okay that last one does sound terrible."
[character_1] sighs, gathering himself, "but that's not what this is. Let me explain."
Cut to the two-shot, decoration swaying. [character_1] says gently "Mother's Day is about your mom. You give her gifts."
[character_2] gasps, delighted, "ahh... gifts for the mummy."
[character_1] groans, "NO—not a mummy—your actual mother!"
[character_2], already committed, "I have prepared. Bandages... sand... a jar labeled DO NOT OPEN."
Cut to close on [character_1], candle flickering. He facepalms, "why would you give your mom a cursed jar?!"
[character_2] shrugs, hat tilting, "it builds character. She will thank me eventually."
[character_1] mutters, "she will absolutely not thank you for a cursed jar."
[character_2] whispers, "then... flowers? Flowers for the tomb?"
Cut back to the two-shot, decoration still swaying. [character_1] snaps "NO TOMB! Just flowers. Nice ones. In a vase."
[character_2] considers this, "a vase... is that a small sarcophagus?"
[character_1], patient now, "it is just a vase. For flowers. That is all."
[character_2] thinks hard, brow furrowing, "so... this Mummy Day... no curses?"
Cut to close on the puppet, candle warm behind. [character_1] (off) answers "no curses." [character_2], "no ancient rituals?"
[character_2] presses on, hopeful, "no summoning? Not even a little summoning?"
[character_1], firm from off-frame, "definitely no summoning, Eldric."
[character_2] slumps, deflated, "...this holiday is deeply disappointing."
Cut to the two-shot, decoration swaying faintly. [character_1] laughs despite himself, "it's normal! it's actually really sweet."
[character_1] softens, "you just call your mom and say thank you. That's the whole thing."
[character_2] tilts his head, "thank you... for what, specifically?"
[character_1] smiles, "for everything. For putting up with you, mostly."
Cut to close on [character_2], hat wobbling as he brightens. "a thank-you. a small, polite, non-cursed thank-you."
[character_2] nods to himself, "I can do that. I am, after all, extremely charming."
[character_1] (off), warm, "there you go. See? Not so hard."
[character_2] pauses, then sheepishly, "...should I still throw out the jar?"
Cut to the two-shot, candle flickering softly. [character_1] says flatly "yes. throw out the jar."
[character_2] sighs dramatically, "fine. but I am keeping the bandages. For emergencies."
[character_1] laughs, shaking his head, "...sure. keep the bandages."
[character_2] puffs up proudly, "then it is settled. I shall honor the Mummy—Mother—with a thank-you."
Cut to close on the two of them, candle soft and steady. [character_1] smiles, "...there you go. You're already a great kid." The flame flickers as the scene settles.

subjects.label: [character_1]

subjects.appearance: light stubble, dark cap, grey hoodie, warm tired face, soft 2D anime style
subjects.voice: low warm conspiratorial voice, slow with dramatic pauses

acts.content: Interior of a parked car at morning, soft light through the windshield, blurred trees outside. [character_1] leans toward the lens and says "hey, come here, I gotta tell you something."
[character_1] glances around like he's sharing a secret, "good coffee... it doesn't have to cost a fortune."
[character_1] settles back, quiet, "for years I overpaid—cafes, subscriptions, the whole thing."
[character_1] shrugs, "and the worst part? I told myself that was just life."
Cut to close, light shifting faintly on the dashboard. [character_1] says "some mornings I'd sit right here before work, just... not ready."
[character_1] looks out the side window, "engine off, phone down. Five minutes that were actually mine."
[character_1] turns back to the lens, "this little routine is the one quiet thing nobody can take."
[character_1] half-smiles, "funny how something this small holds a whole morning together."
Cut to medium, the trees outside swaying faintly. [character_1] nods slowly, "I used to think I needed more."
[character_1] taps the wheel gently, "turns out I needed less. Just... better. Quieter."
[character_1] glances at the empty passenger seat, "my dad drank his coffee black, same mug, thirty years."
[character_1] smiles to himself, "I never got that. Now I kind of do."
Cut to a profile close-up, light soft on his face. [character_1] breathes out, "he wasn't a talker. Coffee was how he sat with himself."
[character_1] quiet, "I think I spent years running from that kind of quiet."
[character_1] lowers his voice, "and now? it's the best part of my day."
[character_1] looks down at his hands, "weird how the boring stuff turns out to matter most."
Cut to medium, trees swaying outside. [character_1] glances out the window, "there's a guy who parks next to me every morning."
[character_1] smiles, "never talked to him. Probably doing the exact same thing."
[character_1] muses, "whole lot full of people, all just... gathering themselves before the day."
[character_1] shakes his head softly, "and we all act like we're the only one who needs it."
Cut to close, light steady on his face. [character_1] leans in, "so here's the secret I came to tell you... slow down. Just for a minute."
[character_1] warm, "the day's gonna be loud enough. Give yourself the quiet first."
[character_1] half-smiles, "and honestly? half the time I'm not telling you. I'm telling me."
[character_1] looks down, "reminding myself the small things still count, even on the heavy days."
Cut to medium, the trees drifting faintly. [character_1] settles, "I had a heavy one yesterday. Sat here twice as long."
[character_1] quiet, "didn't fix anything. Just made it carryable."
[character_1] taps the wheel, "that's all this is, really. A place to set things down for a second."
[character_1] glances at the windshield, "then you pick them back up and go. But lighter."
Cut to a profile close-up, soft morning light. [character_1] breathes out, "my mom always said: rest isn't quitting."
[character_1] smiles, "took me about forty years to believe her."
[character_1] quiet, "I'd call and tell her, but she'd just say 'I told you so.'"
[character_1] chuckles softly, "...maybe I'll call her anyway. After this."
Cut to medium, trees swaying outside. [character_1] turns back, gentle, "so whatever your version of this is—find it."
[character_1] nods, "could be coffee. Could be a walk. Could be just sitting in your car like an idiot."
[character_1] grins, "clearly I recommend the last one."
[character_1] softens, "point is, make it yours. Protect it. Don't let the day steal it."
Cut to close, warm light still. [character_1] looks straight to the lens, "because the day will try. It always tries."
[character_1] quiet, "and five quiet minutes is how you win before it even starts."
[character_1] half-smiles, "alright. that's my whole speech. For real this time."
[character_1] settles back, "go on, have a good one. I mean it."
Cut to medium, trees drifting outside. [character_1] takes a slow breath, content in the quiet cabin.
[character_1] glances at the time, unhurried, "okay. one more minute, then I'm in."
[character_1] smiles to himself, "...maybe two."
[character_1] gives a small nod to the lens, warm and easy, "thanks for sitting with me."
Cut to a final close-up, the morning light warm and still on his face, [character_1] calm as the scene eases to a close.

subjects.label: [character_1]
subjects.name: Dr. Janet
subjects.appearance: neat shoulder-length hair, warm confident face, soft teal cardigan over a blouse, clean 2D anime style
subjects.voice: warm reassuring female voice, clear and steady

acts.content: A calm home-office, soft daylight, a small plant on the desk swaying faintly. [character_1] faces the lens and says warmly "hi, I'm Dr. Janet. Got a minute?"
[character_1] folds her hands, "a lot of people put off taking care of themselves. I get it—life is busy."
[character_1] leans in gently, "but you shouldn't have to choose between your day and your health."
[character_1] nods, "a cold, the flu, allergies—small things that pile up when we ignore them."
Cut to close, the desk plant swaying softly. [character_1] smiles kindly, "you can talk to a real provider in minutes, right from home."
[character_1] warm, "no crowded waiting rooms. No long, exhausting lines."
[character_1] softens, "and if you've been wanting to feel better in your own body—we can help with that too."
[character_1] reassuring, "there's no embarrassment here. Just a conversation, and a plan."
Cut to medium, the curtain edge drifting faintly. [character_1] gestures gently, "a free first consultation, with someone who actually listens."
[character_1] nods, "that's the whole idea. No catch, no script."
[character_1] leans in, "I see it every week—people who waited too long because they thought they had to push through."
[character_1] sincere, "you don't get extra points for suffering quietly. I promise."
Cut to a softer close-up, light gentle and still. [character_1] says "the hardest part is reaching out the first time."
[character_1] warm, "after that, it's just two people figuring out a plan together."
[character_1] softens, "you don't have to be brave about your own health. You just have to show up."
[character_1] smiles, "and you don't have to have it all figured out. That's literally my job."
Cut to medium, the plant swaying softly. [character_1] says "I think we've made health feel like a test you can fail."
[character_1] shakes her head gently, "it isn't. It's just maintenance. Like anything you care about."
[character_1] leans in, "you'd take your car in for a weird noise. Give yourself the same courtesy."
[character_1] smiles, "...okay, you're a lot more important than a car. But you get my point."
Cut to close, warm light steady on her face. [character_1] says "a lot of what I do is just reassurance."
[character_1] warm, "telling someone 'that's normal, here's what we do' and watching them breathe again."
[character_1] softens, "that relief? you deserve it too. Not just everyone you take care of."
[character_1] sincere, "because I'd bet you're the one who never makes the appointment for yourself."
Cut to medium, plant and curtain drifting faintly. [character_1] says "so let's change that today. Just the first step."
[character_1] gentle, "one short conversation. That's where every good plan starts."
[character_1] nods, "no pressure, no judgment. Just a provider who's actually paying attention."
[character_1] warm, "and if it turns out you're fine? wonderful. Now you know, instead of worrying."
Cut to a softer close-up, light gentle and still. [character_1] says "that peace of mind is worth a few minutes."
[character_1] sincere, "I've never once had a patient say 'I wish I'd waited longer.'"
[character_1] smiles softly, "it's always the opposite. 'I should've come sooner.'"
[character_1] warm, "so let this be your sooner. Right now, while you're thinking about it."
Cut to medium, the plant swaying softly. [character_1] says "don't wait until it gets worse."
[character_1] gentle, "the small thing today is so much easier than the big thing later."
[character_1] leans in, "and you're allowed to take up space in your own life. Health included."
[character_1] nods, "reaching out isn't weakness. It's just being a grown-up about it."
Cut to close, warm light steady. [character_1] smiles warmly, "take care of you. You're worth the few minutes."
[character_1] sincere, "the people who count on you need you well, not just running on empty."
[character_1] softens, "so do this for them, if you won't do it for you. Either way, just do it."
[character_1] warm, "and then go be wonderful at everything else."
Cut to medium, plant and curtain drifting faintly. [character_1] says "that's it. That's the whole pep talk."
[character_1] smiles, "no pressure. We're here whenever you're ready."
[character_1] gentle, "even if 'ready' is three weeks from now. We'll still be here."
[character_1] gives a small reassuring nod, "okay. take care of yourself. I mean it."
Cut to a final close-up, the room calm and warm, the plant swaying faintly, [character_1] gentle as the scene eases to a close.

subjects.label: [character_1]
subjects.name: Theo
subjects.appearance: neat short hair, calm thoughtful face, navy crewneck, clean modern 2D anime style
subjects.position: seated at an office desk, center
subjects.voice: calm measured male voice, low and steady

acts.content: A modern office at dusk, soft lamp light, a window blind edge drifting faintly. [character_1] sits at his desk and says quietly "okay. last one in the building. good time to be honest."
[character_1] exhales, "I've been telling everyone the project's fine. It's not, really."
[character_1] looks down at his hands, "we're close, but close has a way of staying close for months."
[character_1] quiet, "and I keep selling the version where it all works out."
Cut to close, the lamp glow pulsing faintly. [character_1] says "funny thing is, I'm not even scared of failing anymore."
[character_1] leans back slightly, "I'm scared of how good I've gotten at pretending it's fine."
[character_1] half-smiles, grim, "that's the part that should worry me. And it does."
[character_1] glances at the dark window, "my mentor used to say: the lie you tell to look calm is the one that costs you."
Cut to medium, the blind edge drifting. [character_1] says "I didn't get it then. I get it tonight."
[character_1] folds his hands, "so tomorrow I'm going to walk in and just... say the real number."
[character_1] nods slowly, "the real timeline. The real risk. No spin."
[character_1] quiet, "maybe they panic. Maybe they don't. But at least it'll be true."
Cut to a profile close-up, lamp glow gentle. [character_1] says "I keep thinking about the version of me that started here three years ago."
[character_1] half-smiles, "that guy would've hated how careful I've become."
[character_1] quiet, "he said what he thought. Sometimes too loud. But you knew where he stood."
[character_1] sighs, "careful isn't the same as honest. I forgot that somewhere."
Cut to medium, the blind edge drifting softly. [character_1] taps the desk once, "alright. I'll write the email tonight while I still mean it."
[character_1] looks to the lens, "because by morning I'll have ten reasons to soften it."
[character_1] firm, "not this time. Send it raw, before the fear edits it."
[character_1] quiet, "if you're sitting late somewhere too, telling yourself it's fine—maybe check if it actually is."
Cut to close, warm light steady. [character_1] says "I think we all have a number we won't say out loud."
[character_1] quiet, "a deadline. A budget. A feeling. The true one, under the polished one."
[character_1] half-smiles, "and the gap between them is exactly how tired you are."
[character_1] breathes out, "mine's been a big gap lately. Tonight I'm closing it."
Cut to medium, the office calm and dim. [character_1] says "that's the whole thought. Nothing dramatic."
[character_1] softens, "I feel lighter already, and I haven't even sent it."
[character_1] looks down, then up, "maybe that's the answer. Say the true thing, then breathe."
[character_1] quiet, "everything I dreaded was the saying. Never the after."
Cut to a profile close-up, lamp glow gentle. [character_1] says "I should call the team in early. Tell them first, before the room."
[character_1] nods, "they've been carrying the weight of my act. They deserve to hear it straight."
[character_1] quiet, "thank the people who kept showing up while I was busy pretending."
[character_1] softens, "they deserved the real me, not the calm act."
Cut to medium, the blind edge drifting once more. [character_1] says "tomorrow I start with the truth and an apology."
[character_1] settles, "and then we fix it together, properly this time."
[character_1] thinks, "no hero saving it at the last second. Just steady, boring honesty."
[character_1] half-smiles, "turns out that's the brave version anyway."
Cut to close, warm and still. [character_1] looks at the dark window a long beat, then back, "funny how quiet a room gets when you stop lying in it."
[character_1] quiet, "good quiet, though. The honest kind. I'd forgotten it."
[character_1] breathes out slowly, "okay. now I'm really done talking myself into it."
[character_1] reaches for the laptop, "email first. Then home. Then actual sleep."
Cut to medium from the original angle, lamp glow soft and pulsing faintly. [character_1] sits quietly, content.
[character_1] pauses over the lamp switch, "...leave it on a minute. I like it here, finally."
[character_1] gives the lens one last small nod, "see you tomorrow. The real one."
[character_1] looks down at the dim desk, shoulders loosening, the warm light holding still around him.
Cut to a wider angle, the whole quiet office in soft lamplight, the blind edge drifting one final time, [character_1] small and still at his desk as the scene eases to a close.

acts.content: 城市天桥夜景，雨后湿漉漉的栏杆映着霓虹，桥下车流拖出红黄光带，远处楼群隐在雨雾里，无人，建立第一处地点
镜头缓缓掠过空荡的天桥面，积水倒映霓虹招牌，雨丝在路灯下细密斜落，桥面尽头一片暗影
[character_1] 从桥的左侧快步走入画面，米白风衣被夜风掀动，她停在栏杆前回头四下张望
[character_1] 扶着冰凉的栏杆，望向桥下流动的车河，呼出一口白气，眉头微蹙似在等谁
[character_2] 从桥的另一端缓缓走近，深灰外套领口竖起，脚步在湿地上踩出轻响，停在几步外
两人隔着几步距离对望，霓虹光在彼此脸上明灭，谁都没有先开口，雨声填满沉默
近景 [character_1] 的脸，她看着 [character_2]，声音被雨打湿，轻声说「你怎么会在这里」
近景 [character_2]，他微微低头又抬起，眼神疲惫，回答「我每天都从这里过，只是没敢叫住你」
镜头绕到两人侧后方，雨丝在霓虹下成帘，两人并肩站到栏杆边，肩膀间留着一道距离
[character_1] 望着桥下车流，轻声说「这一年，我以为再也碰不到你了」，语气里有疲惫也有松动
[character_2] 侧脸看她，喉结动了动，低声答「我也一样，只是一直没找到开口的理由」
镜头从两人身后拉远，整座湿漉的天桥与桥下霓虹车河尽收画面，两个身影在雨夜里渺小并立
地铁站台夜景，空旷站台只有冷白灯光，地面反光如镜，列车进站的风掀动广告布，建立第二处地点
镜头沿着空站台缓缓平移，一排排座椅空着，电子屏闪着到站信息，整个空间冷而寂静
[character_1] 独自站在站台黄线后，米白色风衣下摆被风吹动，望向幽深的隧道口
近景 [character_1] 的侧脸，冷白灯光打在她湿润的眼睫上，她抿着唇似在权衡要不要回头
[character_2] 从扶梯方向走入画面，脚步放轻，停在 [character_1] 身后半米处，没有伸手
[character_2] 看着 [character_1] 的背影，声音压低「上次没说完的话，我想现在说完」
[character_1] 没有回头，肩线微微一颤，轻声答「我在听，这次我哪也不去」
列车呼啸进站，灯光成排扫过两人的脸，车厢风把 [character_1] 的发吹乱，她终于缓缓转身
[character_1] 转过身正面对上 [character_2]，两人在站台中央对站，目光交叠，谁都没动
[character_2] 看着她的眼睛，认真说「当年是我先松的手，这句对不起，我欠了你太久」
[character_1] 眼眶泛红，却笑了一下，低声说「我没要你的对不起，我只想知道你还在不在」
列车门在两人身后开合，乘客模糊地穿行而过，他们却一动不动，像被时间隔开
列车驶离，风声渐息，站台重归冷寂，两人仍并肩立在原地，影子被灯光拉得很长
旧公寓楼道夜景，昏黄声控灯忽明忽暗，斑驳墙面与铁皮信箱，狭窄楼梯向上延伸，建立第三处地点
镜头顺着楼梯向上摇，墙皮剥落处露出旧砖，声控灯应着脚步声一层层亮起又熄灭
[character_1] 走上楼梯，在某一层平台停下，手扶着扶手回望身后的台阶
近景 [character_1] 扶着扶手，米白风衣沾了夜雨的潮气，她垂眼似在数自己走过的这些年
[character_2] 跟着上来，停在低一阶的位置，仰头看着 [character_1]，喘息在安静楼道里清晰可闻
[character_1] 低头看着 [character_2]，轻声问「如果重来一次，你还会走吗」，声音有些发抖
[character_2] 沉默两秒，声音放得很轻很稳「这次我不走了，我陪你走完这段楼梯」
声控灯忽然熄灭，楼道陷入短暂的黑，只剩窗外霓虹漏进的一道微光勾着两人轮廓
灯又应声亮起，光线一明之间，[character_1] 向下走了一阶，与 [character_2] 站到同一台阶上
两人在同一台阶上极近地站着，[character_1] 抬头看他，低声说「那我们，从这层开始」
[character_2] 极轻地点头，回答「好，从这层开始，一层一层往上」，嘴角终于松开
镜头从两人脚边缓缓后退，露出狭窄楼道与向上延伸的台阶，两人并肩立在昏黄灯下
[character_1] 先迈步往上走，[character_2] 跟在半步之后，两人的脚步声在楼道里渐渐合拍
镜头仰拍两人并肩拾级而上的剪影，声控灯一层层在他们前方亮起，像替他们引路
两人在某层窗前停下，窗外是夜雨之城的霓虹海，[character_1] 望向远处那座天桥的方向
近景 [character_1] 望向窗外，轻声说「我们今晚，把这座城走了一整圈」，眼里有光
近景 [character_2] 也望向窗外，低声接「天桥、地铁、这里——以后还会有很多圈」
镜头从两人之间缓缓推向窗外，雨势渐歇，霓虹在湿玻璃上晕成柔和的光斑
两人转身并肩，继续沿楼梯向上走去，背影没入忽明忽暗的灯光，相视一笑
镜头从楼道天井升起俯拍，整座旧公寓的螺旋楼梯里两个小小身影并肩上行，画面定格于雨夜微光

subjects.voice: Low steady male voice, sparse delivery

acts.content: Rooftop high-dive board at night, city lights far below in deep black negative space, a single harsh purple light raking across wet concrete, no people. Establishing the first location
The camera glides slowly along the empty diving board, purple flare smearing on the wet surface, the dark void of the city yawning beyond the edge
[character_1] walks out onto the board, bare feet on the cold wet rim, and stops at the very edge looking down into the dark
Close on [character_1]'s face, eyes open and too calm for the height, purple flare bleeding across the lower frame
[character_1] curls his toes over the wet edge, exhales a slow breath of white vapor, and whispers, "Not down. Through."
[character_1] raises his arms, his lean silhouette stark against the black sky, the whole city holding still beneath him
[character_1] leans forward and dives off the board, body arcing against the black sky, the figure shrinking into darkness
Wide of the empty board after he is gone, purple light still raking the wet concrete, the city lights flickering far below
Flooded subway tunnel, ankle-deep dark water mirroring purple emergency lights, tiled walls dripping, no people. Establishing the second location
The camera drifts down the long flooded corridor, purple emergency lamps doubling in the black water, the only sound a slow steady drip
[character_1] stands waist-deep in the tunnel water, the same wet hair and calm face, staring down the dark corridor
Close on [character_1]'s face under the purple wash, water beading on his skin, his expression unreadable as ever
[character_1] wades forward slowly through the black water, the camera tracking beside him down the tunnel
Close on [character_1]'s hand trailing along the wet tiled wall, a purple ghost outline pulsing once at his fingertips
[character_1] stops, looks back over his shoulder toward the camera, and says quietly, "Still falling."
[character_1] turns and pushes deeper into the dark, the water rippling out behind him in slow purple rings
Wide of [character_1] alone in the long flooded tunnel, his reflection doubling him in the black water below
[character_1] reaches a faint vertical seam of light at the tunnel's end and lifts a hand toward it, the purple grade thinning
Close on [character_1]'s face tilting up into the seam of light, the first hint of warmth touching his cheek
[character_1] steps through the seam, the dark water and purple lamps swallowed behind him into black
Dawn rooftop, the city emerging from night into cold grey-purple first light, shallow puddles on the concrete, no people. Establishing the third location
The camera pans slowly across the bare rooftop, the skyline a flat grey-purple band, the air still and cold before sunrise
[character_1] sits at the rooftop edge, soaked, legs over the void, the same diver, watching the horizon slowly lighten
Close on [character_1]'s face turning toward the rising light, the purple grade slowly warming on his skin
[character_1] exhales a long breath and says quietly to the dawn, "Now I come up."
[character_1] braces his hands on the ledge and pushes himself to his feet, the wet trunks dripping onto the concrete
[character_1] walks slowly along the rooftop edge, the same diver in full light now, the city waking below him
Close on [character_1]'s wet hand resting on the rooftop rail, the purple ghost outline gone, ordinary morning light on his skin
[character_1] turns to face the camera directly and gives a small, tired nod, breaking nothing this time
[character_1] looks back once over the rooftop edge at the drop below, then steps away from it for the first time
Wide of [character_1] alone on the dawn rooftop, the three falls behind him, the grade fully warm at last
The first real sun breaks the horizon and floods the rooftop gold, [character_1] standing still in the new light
Close on [character_1]'s eyes closing into the sun, the tension finally gone from his face, breathing easy
[character_1] opens his eyes and looks out over the waking city, then quietly says, "All the way up."
[character_1] walks toward the rooftop door, his wet footprints trailing behind him on the warm concrete
Close on the drying footprints on the rooftop, the last of the night's water evaporating in the morning sun
[character_1] pauses at the door, hand on the frame, and looks back once at the edge he no longer needs
Close on [character_1]'s face in the doorway, calm and clear, the purple noir grade fully lifted into warm day
[character_1] steps through the door, and for a beat the empty sunlit rooftop holds the frame alone
The camera drifts back across the rooftop to the edge where he sat, only a faint damp mark left on the ledge
Wide of the rooftop and the bright skyline beyond, the city fully awake, no trace of the night's fall remaining
The camera tilts up from the rooftop to the clear morning sky, the last band of purple bleeding out of the grade
A slow aerial drift over the rooftops as the sun climbs, the three locations of the night now all in daylight below
The camera settles on the high-dive board far in the distance, empty and harmless now in the morning light
Final wide crane rising over the bright city, the night's three falls behind, the frame ending on open warm sky

subjects.label: [character_1]
subjects.name: The Commuter
subjects.appearance: Tired man in his forties, grey overcoat, short greying hair, weary eyes, naturalistic documentary look

subjects.label: [character_2]
subjects.name: The Child
subjects.appearance: Small girl around eight, red knit cardigan, brown ponytail, curious bright eyes, naturalistic documentary look

acts.content: Quiet residential street at dawn, horizon in the lower third, long cold shadows, soft film grain, no people. Establishing the first location
The camera drifts down the empty street, parked cars and trimmed hedges in the soft dawn haze, a milk-pale sky overhead
[character_1] exits a house and closes the door, framed in the right third against vertical doorframe lines, buttoning his grey overcoat
Close on [character_1]'s weary face as he checks his watch and sighs, breath fogging faintly in the cold morning air
A yellow note detaches from the door and falls in slow motion past [character_1], who does not notice as he walks off
Extreme close-up of the yellow note landing on the path, paper fibres and soft reflections visible, the handwriting catching light
[character_1] walks away down the street, growing small, his back to the note lying on the pavement behind him
A gust lifts the yellow note off the path and tumbles it toward the street, the camera following it low
Asphalt street, ground level, leading lines from the curb, the note drifting along the gutter. Establishing the second location
Camera ten centimetres above the asphalt following the note as it tumbles and skips along the empty morning street
The note snags on a drain grate for a beat, trembling in the wind, the wet asphalt reflecting a pale sky
The note blurred in the foreground while passersby cross the midground without ever looking down at it
A passing cyclist's wheel just misses the note, the gust of it flipping the paper over and onward down the block
The note tumbles around a corner out of the shadow, the light on the pavement growing warmer ahead
Sunlit street corner, a warm patch of light on the pavement, the note coming to rest in the glow. Establishing the third location
The camera holds on the note glowing in the warm patch of sun, shop awnings and a quiet street behind it
[character_2] walks into the sunlit patch, red cardigan bright in the light, and pauses when she sees the note
[character_2] crouches down, brown ponytail falling forward, and reaches a curious hand toward the yellow paper
Extreme close-up of [character_2]'s small hands lifting the note, warm backlight and soft bokeh behind her fingers
[character_2] reads the note aloud softly, mouthing the words, "Don't forget — you can do this."
Close on [character_2]'s face as she looks up from the note, curiosity turning into a small determined smile
[character_2] stands and looks around the corner, deciding which way the note's owner might have gone
Rear view of [character_2] walking down the sidewalk holding the note, moving through alternating shadow and sunlight
[character_2] reaches a bus stop where a line of tired commuters waits, and she scans their faces holding the note
Close on [character_2] spotting a grey overcoat through the bus window, recognition lighting her eyes
[character_2] steps up toward the open bus doors, clutching the note to her chest, the morning light behind her
Bus interior, cool muted light, condensation on the window, negative space on the left. Establishing the fourth location
The camera moves slowly down the bus aisle past blurred shoulders, the cool light flat on the empty seats
[character_1] sits tired by the bus window, head leaning against the glass, the same grey overcoat, eyes half closed
Close on [character_1]'s reflection ghosting in the condensation-fogged window, the city sliding past behind it
[character_2] steps into the bus aisle and approaches [character_1], holding the yellow note out in front of her
[character_2] stops beside his seat, tugs gently at his sleeve, and the tired man slowly opens his eyes
[character_2] hands the note up to him and says brightly, "Excuse me — I think you dropped this."
Close on [character_1] taking the note from her small hand, his weary eyes dropping to the handwriting
Insert of the note in [character_1]'s hands, the words "you can do this" trembling slightly as his grip tightens
Close on [character_1]'s face as recognition softens it, a slow breath leaving him, his eyes glistening
[character_1] looks up at [character_2] and manages a small smile, "Thank you. I really did need this today."
[character_2] beams up at him, pleased with herself, rocking on her heels in the aisle light
[character_2] gives him a little wave, says "Have a good day," and hops back down the bus aisle
[character_2] hops off the bus at the next stop, the red cardigan vanishing into the bright street outside
Two-shot space now empty beside [character_1], who looks down at the note again, alone but lighter
Close on [character_1] carefully folding the note and tucking it into his breast pocket, over his heart
[character_1] turns to the window, and for the first time this morning the tiredness lifts from his face
The city slides past the fogged bus window, sunlight breaking through, [character_1] watching it go by quietly
Final wide of the bus moving off down the bright avenue, one small yellow note having crossed the whole city to land

subjects.label: [character_1]
subjects.name: 占卜师·薇拉
subjects.appearance: 深色丝绒长袍，披深红头巾，手腕戴多串饰物，神情笃定神秘，烛光暖调
subjects.position: 画面中央，坐在占卜桌后
subjects.voice: 低沉沙哑女声，语速缓慢富有仪式感

acts.content: 昏暗的占卜室，桌上烛火摇曳，铺着深色绒布，一副塔罗牌整齐叠放在桌中央，[character_1] 端坐桌后，建立占卜场景
镜头缓缓推近占卜桌，烛光在牌堆牌背的固定花纹上跳动，[character_1] 的轮廓在暖光里渐渐清晰
[character_1] 双手覆在牌堆上，闭眼低语「静下心来，让命运为你揭示三张牌」
[character_1] 缓缓洗牌，牌面始终朝下，指间的多串饰物随动作轻响，烛光在牌堆上明灭
[character_1] 把牌摊成一道弧线铺在绒布上，抬眼看向求问者方向，沉声说「凭你的直觉，让我替你来抽」
[character_1] 抽出第一张牌横置桌面，缓缓翻开，是「愚者」牌——画面应呈现塔罗牌库中既定的愚者牌图（悬崖边背行囊的旅人）
近景定在愚者牌上停留，牌面悬崖、白狗、行囊的既定图样在烛光下纤毫毕现，[character_1] 的指尖轻轻点上牌缘
[character_1] 沉声解读「愚者，代表你正站在一段全新旅程的起点」，目光从牌面缓缓抬起
[character_1] 身体微微后靠，留白般顿了顿，补充「前方一片未知，但你心里其实并不害怕，对吗」
镜头拉开拍占卜桌全景，第一张愚者牌横陈桌面，[character_1] 的手伸向牌阵，准备抽第二张
[character_1] 抽出第二张牌置于愚者牌右侧，翻开，是「恋人」牌——画面应呈现牌库中既定的恋人牌图（两人立于天使之下）
近景定在恋人牌上，牌面天使、双人、背景山峦的既定图样在烛光里清晰，[character_1] 手指轻抚牌面
[character_1] 缓缓说「恋人，意味着一个重要的选择，也意味着关系里的联结」，语气放得很慢
[character_1] 微微侧头，目光深远，留白演绎道「你最近，是不是正在两条路之间犹豫不决」
镜头平移过愚者与恋人两张并陈的牌，再回到 [character_1] 抽牌的手上，烛火在桌沿投下晃动的影
[character_1] 抽出第三张牌置于最右，翻开，是「塔」牌——画面应呈现牌库中既定的塔牌图（高塔遭雷击崩塌、人影坠落）
近景定在塔牌上，牌面高塔、闪电、坠落人影、火舌的既定图样在烛光下灼灼，[character_1] 神情一凝
[character_1] 压低声音「塔，预示旧的秩序将要崩塌，但崩塌之后，才有重建的可能」
[character_1] 抬眼，留白地补一句「别怕这张牌，它砸碎的，往往是早该松手的东西」
镜头拉远拍三张牌并列的全貌：愚者、恋人、塔，三张既定牌面在绒布上排成一道牌阵
[character_1] 把手掌摊开覆在三张牌上方，缓缓总结「起点、抉择、瓦解——你的牌阵在说，破而后立」
近景再次扫过愚者牌，[character_1] 指尖点在悬崖边旅人上，说「这趟旅程，你已经迈出了第一步」
近景移到恋人牌，[character_1] 续道「中途这个选择，关乎你最在意的那个人，别用脑子，用心」
近景移到塔牌，[character_1] 沉声「至于这座塔，它倒下时，你要做的不是去扶，而是退后一步看清」
[character_1] 抬眼直视镜头，烛光映亮半张脸，留白地问「这三张牌，你听懂了哪一张」
镜头微微后退拍 [character_1] 与三张牌的关系，她双手交叠搁在桌沿，等待无声的回应
[character_1] 拿起愚者牌对着烛火端详，牌面在火光里微微透亮，她轻声说「起点这张，最像现在的你」
[character_1] 把愚者牌放回原位，三张牌的次序丝毫未乱，她抚平绒布，动作仪式般郑重
镜头从桌面缓缓上摇到 [character_1] 的脸，她阖眼片刻，像在替这趟牌阵收尾凝神
[character_1] 睁眼，目光重新落回牌阵，沉声说「命运给了你方向，但走不走，是你自己的事」
